verses.text: After defeat, the people lament that God has cast them off and 'shewed thy people hard things,' making them stagger as with wine. They feel His displeasure. Even God's own may taste bitter setbacks under His hand.

After defeat, the people lament that God has cast them off and 'shewed thy people hard things,' making them stagger as with wine. They feel His displeasure. Even God's own may taste bitter setbacks under His hand.

After defeat, the people lament that God has cast them off and 'shewed thy people hard things,' making them stagger as with wine. They feel His displeasure. Even God's own may taste bitter setbacks under His hand.
Yet He has 'given a banner to them that fear thee,' a rallying point; so they pray, 'save with thy right hand.' Hope rises amid loss. God gives His people a standard to gather under even in defeat.
Yet He has 'given a banner to them that fear thee,' a rallying point; so they pray, 'save with thy right hand.' Hope rises amid loss. God gives His people a standard to gather under even in defeat.
God answers 'in his holiness': the lands are His to apportion, Moab but His washpot, Edom under His shoe. He claims sovereign right. The nations and their borders are wholly at God's disposal.
God answers 'in his holiness': the lands are His to apportion, Moab but His washpot, Edom under His shoe. He claims sovereign right. The nations and their borders are wholly at God's disposal.
God answers 'in his holiness': the lands are His to apportion, Moab but His washpot, Edom under His shoe. He claims sovereign right. The nations and their borders are wholly at God's disposal.
Who will lead them into the fortified city, if not God, who had seemed to abandon them? They look to Him alone. Even when God has chastened, He remains the only hope of victory.
Who will lead them into the fortified city, if not God, who had seemed to abandon them? They look to Him alone. Even when God has chastened, He remains the only hope of victory.

'Give us help from trouble: for vain is the help of man,' they pray, sure that 'through God we shall do valiantly.' Human aid fails. Strength for the battle comes from God, and with Him His people prevail.
